title: The Snubs and Surprises of the Academy Award Nominations
format: Sean and Amanda go through the 93rd Academy Award nominations morning by morning: LaKeith Stanfield's shock supporting-actor nod, Thomas Vinterberg bumping Aaron Sorkin for director, the shutouts for Da 5 Bloods, Ma Rainey's Black Bottom, and One Night in Miami, then a power ranking of the Best Picture field with six weeks to go.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Six nominations, including a genuinely surprising Best Supporting Actor nod for LaKeith Stanfield alongside co-star Daniel Kaluuya, two supporting nominees from a film with two leads.

films.title: The Father
films.note: A Sundance 2020 title almost nobody had actually seen going into nomination morning; recency bias worked in its favor.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: One of the eight Best Picture nominees; the real surprise attached to it was Aaron Sorkin missing a Best Director nomination.

films.title: Minari
films.note: Six nominations, including Youn Yuh-jung for supporting actress and Lee Isaac Chung for director, expected but welcome nonetheless.

films.title: Another Round
films.note: Thomas Vinterberg's surprise Best Director nomination knocked out the presumed Sorkin slot.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Ten nominations, the most of any film this year.

films.title: Borat Subsequent Moviefilm
films.note: Maria Bakalova's supporting actress nomination, plus a Best Adapted Screenplay nod for a movie that's largely improvised.

films.title: The White Tiger
films.note: Ramin Bahrani's Netflix film picked up a surprise Best Adapted Screenplay nomination.

films.title: Eurovision Song Contest: The Story of Fire Saga
films.note: "Husavik" landed a Best Original Song nomination.

films.title: My Octopus Teacher
films.note: The Netflix documentary that wrecked a lot of people during lockdown; not either host's pick, but they get why it connected.

films.title: Ma Rainey's Black Bottom
films.note: Left out of Best Picture despite three acting nominations; Viola Davis, Chadwick Boseman, and George C. Wolfe's staging couldn't carry it in.

films.title: One Night in Miami
films.note: Also shut out of Best Picture; Regina King didn't get a director nomination either, despite praise for how she handles her four leads.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Delroy Lindo's total shutout, save a score nomination, is the sorest spot on the whole slate for both hosts.

films.title: The Mauritanian
films.note: Jodie Foster won a Golden Globe for this and was widely expected in supporting actress; she's not here.

films.title: News of the World
films.note: Four nominations, but nothing for Tom Hanks, Paul Greengrass, or Helena Zengel.

films.title: Hillbilly Elegy
films.note: Glenn Close got in for this; Amy Adams did not, which both hosts count as a relief.

films.title: Boys State
films.note: One of Sean's favorite films of the year, skipped by the documentary branch, likely because almost nobody saw it since it landed on Apple TV+.

films.title: Dick Johnson Is Dead
films.note: Also shut out of documentary; too formally strange for that branch, in Sean's read.

segments.heading: What Happens the Next Six Weeks

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: The presumptive Best Picture winner; Chloe Zhao could become the first person to win four Oscars in one night, for picture, director, adapted screenplay, and editing.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Ten nominations, warm reviews, no enthusiasm. With no editing or original screenplay nod, this could be a repeat of The Irishman's zero-win night.

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Ryan Coogler's first Oscar nomination, as a producer; Sean reads it as an early Spielberg-esque career turn.

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: Five nominations that Sean thinks really add up to one real shot, for Emerald Fennell's original screenplay.

films.title: Sound of Metal
films.note: Six nominations for a film that premiered at Toronto back in 2019 and built its support slowly online; Amanda's still not sure why it clicked this hard.

films.title: Pieces of a Woman
films.note: Vanessa Kirby's lone nomination for the film; a win here would stun both hosts.

films.title: The United States vs. Billie Holiday
films.note: Andra Day's nomination is its only one.

segments.heading: Power-Ranking Best Picture
segments.note: least to most likely to win, per Sean
segments.subject: Best Picture

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: No. 1 - the consensus front-runner for three months running.

films.title: Minari
films.note: No. 2 - still winning people over as more of them see it.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: No. 3

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: No. 4

films.title: Sound of Metal
films.note: No. 5

films.title: Promising Young Woman
films.note: No. 6

films.title: The Father
films.note: No. 7

films.title: Mank
films.note: No. 8 - Sean's call; Amanda would move it up to sixth.
